title: Patch the Checkout Quantity Flaw
objective: Patch checkout to reject invalid (negative or zero) quantities.
briefing: Logs show a checkout call with a negative quantity that resulted in an account credit instead of a charge. The endpoint never validates that quantity is a positive number.
hints: Restarting the web server won't add a validation rule that was never written.
The fix needs to reject the request before it ever computes a charge.
debrief: The patch adds a server-side check that rejects any checkout request where quantity isn't a positive integer, before the charge/credit calculation ever runs. This closes the specific bug, but the deeper lesson for Insecure Design (A04:2021) is process, not just code: business logic needs abuse-case threat modeling at design time (what if this number is negative, zero, huge, non-numeric?) rather than discovering each one after an attacker finds it.

logs: [LOGIC] checkout processed negative quantity -5 - account credited $50.00

patch /var/www/api/checkout.php --enforc.output:
patched /var/www/api/checkout.php
adding validation: quantity must be > 0... done

logs_append: [FIX] checkout now rejects quantity <= 0 before charge calculation
